titleStmt.title: SII 23.570: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 570. (A.R. No. 570 of 1907.) Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District Saumyanātha temple—on the south base of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine

summary.p: This begins with the Sanskrit introduction Charaṇasarōruha etc. It records an order of (the Telugu Chōḍa chief) Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa Nallam Siddharasa to the Mahājanas of Nirandaṉūr which he calls his brahmapuri, restoring to them the village within the four boundaries, which had been originally granted to them by Vatsarāja and Siddharasa's ancestors and declaring that the Veḷḷāḷas (probably then in illegal occupation) had no title to the village. The charter of possession is said to have been made by the chief in the presence of god Śokkapperumāḷ. There seems to be some gap in the body of the record.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti caraṇasaroruha vihita pilocana trilocana pramukhākhila pṛthviśvarakārita kāveritira karikālakularatnapradipāhita kumārāmku
śayanasiṅha kāñcīpura maheśvara śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaleśvara nmaturān=takap pot tappiccoḻa nallam cittaracanen
svasti śrīmaturān=takap pottappiccoḻa nallam cittarasaneṉ Eṉ brahmapuriyāna niran=taṉūr mahājanattukku śilāśāsanam paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi muṉpu vatsarājarum Eṉ vamsattārum kuṭuttapaṭiye nāṉum Eṉpiḷḷaikaḷum Ivvūr catussimaikkum naṭuvu
m Ivakaḷukku svammāka cokkapperumāḷ tirumuṉpe dhārāpūrvaka mākak kuṭutteṉ veḷḷāḷaṟkku Ivvūril prāpti Illai Ippaṭikku śilāśāsanam paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ Itt(ā)ṉmattukku Aḻivu ceyvār gaṅgai Iṭai kumari Iṭai kurālpacu kuttiṉāl pāvattil paṭuvāṉ . svadattām paradattām vā yo hareti vasundharām ṣaṣṭim varṣa sahasrāṇi Ivai ketāramuṭaiyā neḻuttu Ivai centāṇṭi Eḻuttu
Ippaṭikkivai maturān=takap pottappic coḻa nallañ cittiyaraca neḻuttu Ivai can=tiracūṭi cittaya neḻuttu Ivai ce neḻuttu Ivai rāja niyokattāl jotiṣap perumā neḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 23.570 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
